subject: [PATCH] mm: constify oom_control, scan_control, and alloc_context nodemask

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary: Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes suggested adding implicit caveats to the patch description, specifically that it's about this actual patch and not something unrelated.
reviews.raw_body:
I would add caveats of:

- Complains legitimately
- And it's about this actual patch not something unrelated

:P

(Not speaking for Zi of course, but I mean just in general I feel these caveats
should be implicit :))

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is a good step towards improving const correctness in the Linux kernel and successfully built on their local system, thus giving it a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for doing this, it's nice to gradually up our const correctness game
(as much as C can ever be const correct :)

LGTM, builds locally too, so:

Reviewed-by: Lorenzo Stoakes <ljs@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer Zi Yan pointed out that the const qualifier is not sufficient to prevent modification of nodemask_t pointers, and suggested using a const pointer to nodemask_t instead.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author responded to a comment about his AI review tool by jokingly stating that questioning its legitimacy is grounds for excommunication and making a humorous remark about getting used to the tool.
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you for your contribution! Gourry AI review found 1 potential issue(s) to consider:
- [High] Questioning the legitimacy of AI is grounds for excommunication
--

[Severity: High]
Insolence will not be suffered, take this man away.

;]

(fwiw I'm getting used to Sashiko, it's doing better)

~Gregory

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledges that there may be more work needed on the patch, which has been carried locally for a year in private nodes work.
reviews.raw_body:
I've actually been carrying this patch locally for about a year in the
private nodes work.  Sorry i didn't send it sooner.  There is likely
more work to be done here.

reviews.author: SeongJae Park
reviews.summary:
Gave Tested-by, Acked-by

Reviewer noted inconsistent indentation in the patch, specifically mentioning that some lines use only spaces while others have both spaces and tabs.
reviews.raw_body:
Seems the above indentation has changed for a rason that I have no clue, and
also introduced a line having both spaces and tabs.

Just thinking loud.


Thanks,
SJ

[...]

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch's constification of nodemask_t pointers aligns with existing kernel conventions, specifically matching the alignment of gfp_t on preceding lines.
reviews.raw_body:
It aligns the const with gfp_t on the line above.

This is a common alignment throughout the kernel for any function whose
prototype spans multiple lines.

Thanks
~Gregory

2026-06-09.patch_summary: This patch makes the nodemasks in several Linux kernel structures const, ensuring they are not modified when passed around between contexts. The affected structures include oom_control, scan_control, and alloc_context. This change is motivated by the fact that these nodemasks can come from various sources, including tasks and cpusets, and should be treated as immutable. The patch modifies 11 files across several kernel directories to make this change.
